text: After you finish and place your poem on the stack, Dirtus reads all four in sequence. His voice changes when he reads other people's words — slower, careful.

Then he adds his own. His poem is the longest. It's about what a herald does when the king hasn't arrived yet: you keep announcing anyway. You trust the arrival is coming. You make the announcement so good that when the king shows up, they feel welcomed.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I've been here two years and I've never — we've never done anything like this. All four weeks at once. All five of us. Together.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I don't know if Scatha is real. I still don't know. But I know this is real.
He gestures at the stack of five poems.
Nobody says anything for a moment. Then Vermia nods, very seriously.
